Our story

A Goodland
institution
for 46 years

Nestled on the waterfront in the charming fishing village of Goodland, Little Bar has been a beloved local tradition since 1978. Known for fresh, local seafood, quality steaks, and a relaxed friendly atmosphere.

Dine indoors, on the screened lanai, or in the lush waterside patios — then stay for live entertainment in the cozy bar. The best in Collier County.

Waterfront Patio Screened Lanai Live Entertainment Fresh Local Seafood Quality Steaks
View Full Menu
Fresh from the water

The stars of
our kitchen

Seasonal Specialty

Stone Crab Claws

Caught fresh from local waters. Served daily throughout the season based on availability — order early, they go fast.

Chef's Selection

Fresh Catch Daily

Grouper, Soft Shell Crab, Swordfish — the menu follows what the boats bring in. Always fresh, always local.

Nightly Special

Prime Rib

Slow-roasted and carved nightly. The landsman's answer to a great seafood house — always on our Specials Menu.

46 years & counting

A place where
memories
are made

Little Bar isn't just a restaurant — it's part of Goodland's soul. The original Kossett artwork above has watched over this waterfront corner since before most of our guests were born.

Stone Crab season brings the faithful back year after year. The dock lights shimmer at dusk. The band plays. This is what Florida was before the condos arrived.
